title: Thread allowedCudaVersions through Pod create (driver-floor guard) - REST + GraphQL
description:
GAP FOUND (2026-07-04, during MPI-187). Our Pod create spec sends NO CUDA-version filter, so RunPod can place a Pod on a host whose driver is too old for the image's CUDA/torch build -> ComfyUI crashes on boot with 'RuntimeError: The NVIDIA driver on your system is too old' (seen live on runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0 landing on a CUDA-12.8-max host). Pod placement is currently driver-roulette.

=== FIX ===
RunPod supports a hard placement filter: allowedCudaVersions (array, e.g. ["12.8"] or ["13.0"]). RunPod only lands a host whose driver supports one of the listed CUDA versions. Thread it through BOTH create paths, mirroring the existing minMemoryInGb plumbing (MPI-160):
  1. remotePodLifecycle.js _createPodInternal (~L390): accept an allowedCudaVersions param; set spec.allowedCudaVersions when present (GPU Pods only - N/A for CPU download mode).
  2. runpodRemote.js createPod: REST POST /pods already forwards the whole spec -> just include the field. Verify REST accepts it (live probe per tool_runpod_live_api_probe pattern).
  3. runpodRemote.js createPodGraphql (~L172): thread allowedCudaVersions into the GraphQL `input` the SAME way minMemoryInGb is threaded (~L190): `if (Array.isArray(spec.allowedCudaVersions) && spec.allowedCudaVersions.length) input.allowedCudaVersions = spec.allowedCudaVersions;`. Confirm PodFindAndDeployOnDemandInput accepts it.

=== WHERE THE VALUE COMES FROM (SIMPLIFIED post-MPI-189) ===
UPDATE 2026-07-04: MPI-189 collapses the two-profile split into ONE cu130 image, so this is now a SINGLE constant, not a per-profile map: allowedCudaVersions = ["13.0"] for the cu130 image. Set it once in _createPodInternal for GPU Pods (N/A for CPU download mode). No podImageForCard-keyed lookup needed (that branching is deleted by MPI-189). If MPI-189 slips and both profiles still exist temporarily, key off the resolved image tag (cu130 -> ["13.0"]); otherwise it's a flat constant.

=== WHY IT MATTERS ===
- Immediate: guards against the driver-too-old boot crash on the CURRENT cu128 image (a host with a <12.8 driver would crash today; unguarded).
- HARD PREREQ for cu130: if MPI-187 greenlights a cu130 image rebuild (fault-in fix), shipping it WITHOUT this guard = every 5090/Blackwell Pod is driver-roulette. Must land before any cu130 image ships.

TEST: live-probe both APIs accept the field (temp route + curl per tool_runpod_live_api_probe, delete after). Then a real create with allowedCudaVersions:["13.0"] should only land r580+ hosts; with a deliberately-impossible value should SUPPLY_CONSTRAINT (like the minMemoryInGb 200GB proof).

BRANCH: RunPod. Cross-ref: MPI-187 (found it), MPI-160 (minMemoryInGb precedent - identical plumbing), MPI-70 (two-profile CUDA-floor image split), docs/runpod-remote-engine.md.
